notes: Rebuilt 2026-07-21 — the prior fixture invoice (#14080) no longer existed on job 4304 (staging data drift) and every invoice amount used by this suite has been capped at $10 or below per policy. Invoice #17630 (Gross $10, invoice-level Retainage % override to 10%) was created and approved live via MCP browser specifically because 10% on $10 yields a clean whole-dollar Retainage Withheld ($1) with zero rounding ambiguity anywhere (line-items grid, invoice list, Invoice Details Overview panel, and the Math.round-to-nearest-dollar check in TC329/TC330) — smaller overrides (e.g. 4%) produce sub-$1 withheld amounts that the Overview panel (but not the grid) rounds down to $0 on display, which looked like a bug but isn't. If this fixture invoice is ever deleted, recreate one the same way (small whole-dollar-clean Retainage Withheld) and update invoiceId here — TC22_Retainage.spec.js and pages/retainagePage.js read only from this file, no code changes needed.

editContractOverview.lockedMessage: Contract is finalized. Retainage % cannot be changed.

patterns.overDrawErrorMessage: ^Failed to confirm invoice: Retainage released \(\$([\d,]+\.\d{2})\) cannot exceed retainage withheld \(\$([\d,]+\.\d{2})\) for a contract line\. Reduce the release before approving\.$
